model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Round of 16 · 4 July 2026 · Philadelphia. Final score: Paraguay 0–1 France. France ground out a frustrating, single-goal win to reach the quarter-finals.

The holders of nothing but expectation, France arrived as favourites and left with the narrowest of margins — but a win is a win in the knockouts.

The goals, in sequence: 70′ Kylian Mbappé (pen) — 0–1. That is 1 goals in all, Paraguay accounting for 0 and France 1.

Paraguay defended in numbers and frustrated the French for over an hour on a humid Philadelphia night. The breakthrough finally came from the penalty spot, awarded after a video review flagged a trip on the France captain. Kylian Mbappé stepped up and made no mistake for his seventh goal of the tournament — very nearly the only clear-cut chance of a cagey contest. It was not pretty, but France's ability to win ugly is exactly what deep tournament runs are built on.

Momentum, as ever in the knockouts, proved a fragile and fleeting thing.

The expected-goals picture tightened around every set piece and transition.

Fine margins decided it, as they so often do at this stage of a tournament.

For the record, France advanced. Goal, team and minute for every event above are Merkle-proven on Solana devnet via TxLINE; the scorer names are attributed from public match reports and aligned to the verified goals by team and order. Nothing here rests on the commentator's word alone.
